channel.title: Kyunolab Mystery Archive
channel.description: Legends, folklore, mysteries, and strange tales.

item.title: Black Cat Superstition Origin: Luck, Witchcraft, and How an Animal Became an Omen
item.description: Black cat superstition does not have one single origin. Different cultures have treated black cats as bad luck, good luck, witchcraft companions, protectors, or omens depending on place and period.

item.title: Ring of Gyges: Invisibility, Power, and the Mythic Object That Tests Justice
item.description: The Ring of Gyges is a story in Plato Republic about a ring that grants invisibility. The tale asks whether a person would remain just if no one could see or punish them.

item.title: Oracle of Delphi: Sacred Place, Prophecy, and the Voice Beneath Apollo
item.description: The Oracle of Delphi was the most famous ancient Greek oracle, associated with Apollo and the priestess known as the Pythia. People came to Delphi seeking responses to political, personal, and religious questions.

item.title: Green Flash at Sunset: Optical Phenomenon, Horizon Lore, and the Light People Wait For
item.description: The Green Flash is a brief optical phenomenon sometimes seen near the upper edge of the sun at sunrise or sunset. It is linked to atmospheric refraction and viewing conditions near a clear horizon.

item.title: Agartha Hollow Earth Legend: Hidden Realm, Inner World, and the Geography Beneath the Map
item.description: Agartha, also spelled Agharta in some sources, is a hidden-world legend often connected with hollow-earth and esoteric geography. It is imagined as an inner realm, secret civilization, or spiritual center beneath ordinary maps.

item.title: Basilisk Folklore: Deadly Gaze, Serpent King, and the Creature No One Should Meet
item.description: The basilisk is a mythic creature often described as a deadly serpent or serpent-like king whose gaze, breath, or touch can kill. Medieval and later traditions sometimes blend it with rooster-serpent imagery related to the cockatrice.

item.title: Phaethon Sun Chariot Myth: Fire, Fall, and the Danger of Driving the Sun
item.description: Phaethon is a Greek mythic figure who asks to drive the chariot of the sun and loses control. The burning path threatens the world until Zeus strikes him down.

item.title: Beast of Bray Road: Wisconsin Sightings, Werewolf Shape, and a Modern Monster Legend
item.description: The Beast of Bray Road is a modern Wisconsin legend about a wolf-like or werewolf-like creature reported near Bray Road and the Elkhorn area. Witnesses describe a large animal or upright figure with canine features.

item.title: Green Children of Woolpit: Medieval Wonder, Strange Arrival, and the Folklore of Two Children
item.description: The Green Children of Woolpit is a medieval English story about two children with green skin who appeared near Woolpit, spoke an unknown language, and at first would eat only beans.

item.title: Taos Hum Mystery: Low Sound, Witness Reports, and the Noise No One Fully Explains
item.description: The Taos Hum is a reported low sound heard by some people around Taos, New Mexico. Witnesses describe a persistent hum, drone, or vibration that not everyone can hear.

item.title: Bhangarh Fort Legend: Ruins, Warnings, and the Haunted Place Story of Rajasthan
item.description: Bhangarh Fort is a ruined fort in Rajasthan widely associated with haunted-place legends. Retellings connect the site with curses, forbidden nighttime entry, and stories of a settlement that became abandoned.

item.title: This Man Dream Hoax: The Face, the Website, and the Internet Legend of Shared Dreams
item.description: This Man is an internet legend built around a composite-looking face and the claim that many unrelated people had seen him in dreams. The story spread through posters, websites, forums, and image sharing.

item.title: Clown Statue Urban Legend: Babysitter, Unwanted Object, and the Figure in the Room
item.description: The Clown Statue is a modern urban legend about a babysitter who notices a clown statue or doll in the house, only to learn that the family does not own one. The object becomes frightening because it has been present in the room while being treated as harmless.

item.title: Vanishing Hitchhiker Urban Legend: The Passenger Who Disappears Before Arrival
item.description: The Vanishing Hitchhiker is a widely known urban legend about a driver who picks up a quiet passenger on a lonely road. The passenger disappears before the ride ends, or the driver later learns that the person died years earlier.

item.title: Polybius Arcade Game Legend: The Missing Cabinet, Rumor, and Internet Folklore
item.description: Polybius is an internet folklore story about a mysterious arcade game supposedly tested in the early 1980s. The legend says the cabinet caused strange physical or psychological effects, then disappeared.

item.title: Bennington Triangle: Disappearances, Mountain Roads, and a Vermont Place Legend
item.description: The Bennington Triangle is a regional place legend connected with disappearances around Glastenbury Mountain and the Bennington area of Vermont. The name groups several events into one mysterious landscape.

item.title: Oak Island Money Pit: Treasure, Tunnels, and the Mystery That Refuses to End
item.description: The Oak Island Money Pit is a famous Nova Scotia mystery about a supposed buried treasure site discovered in the late eighteenth century. Later stories describe platforms, flood tunnels, strange finds, and repeated digs.

item.title: Wild Hunt Folklore: Ghostly Riders, Storm Skies, and the Sound of a Passing Host
item.description: The Wild Hunt is a European folklore motif about a supernatural host, often heard or seen moving through the night sky, winter weather, or storm. It may appear as ghostly riders, hunters, hounds, or a procession of the dead.

item.title: Men in Black Legend: UFO Witnesses, Silent Visitors, and Modern Conspiracy Folklore
item.description: The Men in Black legend describes strange dark-suited visitors who allegedly appear after UFO sightings, ask questions, and warn witnesses to keep quiet. The figures are usually calm, intimidating, and slightly wrong.

item.title: Prometheus Fire Myth: Theft, Punishment, and the Gift That Changed Humanity
item.description: Prometheus is a Greek mythic figure remembered for stealing fire and giving it to humanity. Zeus punishes him for crossing divine limits, often by binding him while an eagle tears at him.
